businessModel.text: Revenue from NIKE brand ( footwear 46%, apparel 33%, equipment 6%) through DTC (owned stores and digital) and wholesale channels. Geographically: North America 38%, Europe/Middle East/Africa 26%, Greater China 17%, Asia Pacific/Latin America 19%.

recentCatalysts.text: New CEO Elliott Hill appointed October 2024 — immediate wholesale channel reset initiated Air Innovation pipeline: Air Max 2025 and renewed Jordan franchise in FY26 Nike University internal talent programme re-energising product creation teams Cost-savings programme: $2bn efficiency target by FY27 (supply chain + SG&A)
items: New CEO Elliott Hill appointed October 2024 — immediate wholesale channel reset initiated
Air Innovation pipeline: Air Max 2025 and renewed Jordan franchise in FY26
Nike University internal talent programme re-energising product creation teams
Cost-savings programme: $2bn efficiency target by FY27 (supply chain + SG&A)

keyRisks.text: 1. Revenue decline FY25 (-1.8%) driven by North America wholesale exit 2. Gross margin at 44.2% FY25 vs 47.6% FY22 — structural compression from DTC channel shift 3. Chinese competitor Anta Sports gaining market share at a double-digit rate 4. Jordan Brand licensed revenue at risk of re-negotiation (2026 franchise renewal) 5. Inventory glut in North America still being worked through Q4 FY26

risks.risk: 1. Revenue decline FY25 (-1.8%) driven by North America wholesale exit
2. Gross margin at 44.2% FY25 vs 47.6% FY22 — structural compression from DTC channel shift
3. Chinese competitor Anta Sports gaining market share at a double-digit rate
4. Jordan Brand licensed revenue at risk of re-negotiation (2026 franchise renewal)
5. Inventory glut in North America still being worked through Q4 FY26

whoShouldOwn.text: Turnaround play for patient investors who believe in the new CEO's strategic reset. Brand-revival optionality on new product cycle. NOT suitable for risk-off macro investors or those requiring near-term revenue positive revisions.

entryExit.text: Turnaround speculation — accumulate $38–$44. HOLD $44–$55. REDUCE above $55. Track: Jordan franchise renewal outcome and Q4 FY26 inventory clearance progress. | BUY: Turnaround speculation — accumulate $38–$44. | REDUCE above $55.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
